model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Vanderbilt is one of the strongest 5-seeds in the bracket, ranking 10th-12th nationally with an adjusted efficiency margin of +28.12, nearly 16 points higher than McNeese's +12.52, driven by the nation's 6th-ranked offense (AdjO 127.3). The 11.5-point spread reflects a wider quality gap than the typical 5/12 matchup, as Vanderbilt's SEC-tested roster—fresh off an SEC Tournament finals run—possesses significantly more firepower than McNeese's Southland Conference champions can match. While the 5/12 line historically produces upsets roughly 35% of the time, McNeese's offense (91st nationally) lacks the elite scoring punch needed to keep pace with Vanderbilt's top-10 attack, and the Cowboys' 28-5 record was built against dramatically weaker competition.

reasoningStep.title: South 5/12: Vanderbilt vs McNeese
reasoningStep.summary: Vanderbilt's elite offensive profile (KenPom #12, AdjO 6th nationally) and ~16-point efficiency margin advantage over McNeese make them a strong favorite despite the historically upset-prone 5/12 seed line. The 11.5-point spread confirms this is a wider gap than typical 5/12 games.
evidence: Vanderbilt: KenPom #12, Torvik #10, AdjEM +28.12, AdjO 127.3 (6th), AdjD 99.2 (28th), 26-7 record in SEC
McNeese: KenPom #67, AdjEM +12.52, AdjO 114.3 (91st), AdjD 101.8, 28-5 in Southland Conference
Efficiency margin gap of ~15.6 points per 100 possessions is significantly wider than typical 5/12 matchups
Vanderbilt favored by 11.5 points; made SEC Tournament championship game before losing to Arkansas 86-75
McNeese won Southland Conference regular season and tournament titles but schedule strength is far below SEC level

result.text: Get To Know The 2026 Southland Tournament Champs: McNeese Basketball Skip to content CBB Review (College Basketball Review) College Basketball Power Rankings, Interviews and more! ACC Boston College Cal Clemson Duke Florida State Georgia Tech Louisville Miami (Fla) NC State North Carolina Notre Dame Pitt SMU Stanford Syracuse Virginia Virginia Tech Wake Forest Big East Butler Creighton DePaul Georgetown Marquette Providence Seton Hall St. John’s UConn Villanova Xavier Big Ten Illinois Indiana Iowa Maryland Michigan Michigan State Minnesota Nebraska Northwestern Ohio State Oregon Penn State Purdue Rutgers UCLA USC Washington Wisconsin Big 12 Arizona Arizona State Baylor BYU Cincinnati Colorado Houston Iowa State Kansas Kansas State Oklahoma State TCU Texas Tech UCF Utah West Virginia SEC Alabama Arkansas Auburn Florida Georgia Kentucky LSU Mississippi State Missouri Oklahoma Ole Miss South Carolina Tennessee Texas Texas A&M Vanderbilt Mid Majors AAC Atlantic Sun Atlantic 10 C-USA Horizon MAAC MAC Missouri Valley Mountain West SoCon WAC WCC Oregon State Washington State Bracketology Matty Brackets March on CBB Review Rankings Meet Our Staff Get To Know The 2026 Southland Tournament Champs: McNeese Basketball By georgebagwell Mar 16, 2026 McNeese Basketball Logo McNeese basketball is dancing yet again, riding a wave through Lake Charles to go back to the NCAA tournament. No Will Wade? No problem for McNeese, who weathered a coaching change to put up quite the season in the Southland again. The Cowboys are one of the most impressive mid-majors in the sport for the second season in a row, and they’ve got the talent to prove it. How they got there: For the first time ever, the Southland Conference made the jump up to a 22-game conference schedule. This meant McNeese had just nine non-conference games on their docket, and their first conference game came on the first day of December. While that game was a shock loss on the road to Incarnate Word, the Cowboys mostly took care of business from that point against Southland opponents, going 19-2 in their final 21 games in-conference. But that wasn’t enough to secure the one seed in the Southland tournament . Stephen F. Austin, who split the season series with McNeese, only lost one other game against a conference opponent, a questionable home loss to New Orleans. The Lumberjacks earned the top spot with a 20-2 record, setting up a potential crash course with McNeese in the final, with the first two seeds earning byes to the semifinals. McNeese had to get past UTRGV first, which was no small task. The Vaqueros had won 13 of their past 15 games, including one over McNeese at home. In an instant classic, the Cowboys prevailed 84-80 in triple overtime, making not one, not two, but three game-tying attempts with under a minute to go across regulation and the first and second overtimes. Larry Johnson punctuated the win with a dunk with seconds to go. Stephen F. Austin needed some late magic of t

result.text: McNeese vs. Vanderbilt (Mar 19, 2026) Pregame - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search Vanderbilt Commodores vs McNeese Cowboys NCAA Men's Basketball Championship - South Region - 1st Round 12 McNeese Cowboys McNeese MCN 28-5 truTV 5 Vanderbilt Commodores Vanderbilt VAN 26-8 Gamecast Preview Videos Bracket Tickets External Link Men's Tournament Challenge Vanderbilt faces McNeese in opening round of NCAA Tournament By Data Skrive Mar 18, 2026, 04:46 am Facebook Facebook Messenger Twitter Email McNeese Cowboys (28-5, 21-3 Southland) vs. Vanderbilt Commodores (26-8, 13-8 SEC) Oklahoma City; Thursday, 3:15 p.m. EDT Commodores -11.5; over/under is 150.5 BOTTOM LINE: No. 16 Vanderbilt plays in the first round of the NCAA Tournament against McNeese. The Commodores are 13-8 against SEC opponents and 13-0 in non-conference play. Vanderbilt is 2-2 in one-possession games. The Cowboys' record in Southland action is 21-3. McNeese is the best team in the Southland scoring 16.0 fast break points per game. Vanderbilt makes 47.5% of its shots from the field this season, which is 6.8 percentage points higher than McNeese has allowed to its opponents (40.7%). McNeese averages 80.0 points per game, 4.8 more than the 75.2 Vanderbilt gives up to opponents. TOP PERFORMERS: Tyler is averaging 19.1 points, 5.1 assists and 2.4 steals for the Commodores. Duke Miles is averaging 13.1 points and 3.0 rebounds while shooting 42.6% over the past 10 games. Larry Johnson is scoring 17.5 points per game and averaging 5.5 rebounds for the Cowboys. Tyshawn Archie is averaging 2.3 made 3-pointers over the last 10 games. LAST 10 GAMES: Commodores: 6-4, averaging 80.8 points, 30.6 rebounds, 14.5 assists, 6.8 steals and 3.4 blocks per game while shooting 45.0% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 78.6 points per game. Cowboys: 10-0, averaging 77.2 points, 30.1 rebounds, 13.8 assists, 10.8 steals and 3.7 blocks per game while shooting 46.1% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 64.0 points. ------ The Associated Press created this story using technology provided by Data Skrive and data from Sportradar . result.text: Get To Know The 2026 Southland Tournament Champs: McNeese Basketball Skip to content CBB Review (College Basketball Review) College Basketball Power Rankings, Interviews and more! ACC Boston College Cal Clemson Duke Florida State Georgia Tech Louisville Miami (Fla) NC State North Carolina Notre Dame Pitt SMU Stanford Syracuse Virginia Virginia Tech Wake Forest Big East Butler Creighton DePaul Georgetown Marquette Providence Seton Hall St. John’s UConn Villanova Xavier Big Ten Illinois Indiana Iowa Maryland Michigan Michigan State Minnesota Nebraska Northwestern Ohio State Oregon Penn State Purdue Rutgers UCLA USC Washington Wisconsin Big 12 Arizona Arizona State Baylor BYU Cincinnati Colorado Houston Iowa State Kansas Kansas State Oklahoma State TCU Texas Tech UCF Utah West Virginia SEC Alabama Arkansas Auburn Florida Georgia Kentucky LSU Mississippi State Missouri Oklahoma Ole Miss South Carolina Tennessee Texas Texas A&M Vanderbilt Mid Majors AAC Atlantic Sun Atlantic 10 C-USA Horizon MAAC MAC Missouri Valley Mountain West SoCon WAC WCC Oregon State Washington State Bracketology Matty Brackets March on CBB Review Rankings Meet Our Staff Get To Know The 2026 Southland Tournament Champs: McNeese Basketball By georgebagwell Mar 16, 2026 McNeese Basketball Logo McNeese basketball is dancing yet again, riding a wave through Lake Charles to go back to the NCAA tournament. No Will Wade? No problem for McNeese, who weathered a coaching change to put up quite the season in the Southland again. The Cowboys are one of the most impressive mid-majors in the sport for the second season in a row, and they’ve got the talent to prove it. How they got there: For the first time ever, the Southland Conference made the jump up to a 22-game conference schedule. This meant McNeese had just nine non-conference games on their docket, and their first conference game came on the first day of December. While that game was a shock loss on the road to Incarnate Word, the Cowboys mostly took care of business from that point against Southland opponents, going 19-2 in their final 21 games in-conference. But that wasn’t enough to secure the one seed in the Southland tournament . Stephen F. Austin, who split the season series with McNeese, only lost one other game against a conference opponent, a questionable home loss to New Orleans. The Lumberjacks earned the top spot with a 20-2 record, setting up a potential crash course with McNeese in the final, with the first two seeds earning byes to the semifinals. McNeese had to get past UTRGV first, which was no small task. The Vaqueros had won 13 of their past 15 games, including one over McNeese at home. In an instant classic, the Cowboys prevailed 84-80 in triple overtime, making not one, not two, but three game-tying attempts with under a minute to go across regulation and the first and second overtimes. Larry Johnson punctuated the win with a dunk with seconds to go. Stephen F. Austin needed some late magic of t

result.text: McNeese vs. Vanderbilt (Mar 19, 2026) Pregame - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search Vanderbilt Commodores vs McNeese Cowboys NCAA Men's Basketball Championship - South Region - 1st Round 12 McNeese Cowboys McNeese MCN 28-5 truTV 5 Vanderbilt Commodores Vanderbilt VAN 26-8 Gamecast Preview Videos Bracket Tickets External Link Men's Tournament Challenge Vanderbilt faces McNeese in opening round of NCAA Tournament By Data Skrive Mar 18, 2026, 04:46 am Facebook Facebook Messenger Twitter Email McNeese Cowboys (28-5, 21-3 Southland) vs. Vanderbilt Commodores (26-8, 13-8 SEC) Oklahoma City; Thursday, 3:15 p.m. EDT Commodores -11.5; over/under is 150.5 BOTTOM LINE: No. 16 Vanderbilt plays in the first round of the NCAA Tournament against McNeese. The Commodores are 13-8 against SEC opponents and 13-0 in non-conference play. Vanderbilt is 2-2 in one-possession games. The Cowboys' record in Southland action is 21-3. McNeese is the best team in the Southland scoring 16.0 fast break points per game. Vanderbilt makes 47.5% of its shots from the field this season, which is 6.8 percentage points higher than McNeese has allowed to its opponents (40.7%). McNeese averages 80.0 points per game, 4.8 more than the 75.2 Vanderbilt gives up to opponents. TOP PERFORMERS: Tyler is averaging 19.1 points, 5.1 assists and 2.4 steals for the Commodores. Duke Miles is averaging 13.1 points and 3.0 rebounds while shooting 42.6% over the past 10 games. Larry Johnson is scoring 17.5 points per game and averaging 5.5 rebounds for the Cowboys. Tyshawn Archie is averaging 2.3 made 3-pointers over the last 10 games. LAST 10 GAMES: Commodores: 6-4, averaging 80.8 points, 30.6 rebounds, 14.5 assists, 6.8 steals and 3.4 blocks per game while shooting 45.0% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 78.6 points per game. Cowboys: 10-0, averaging 77.2 points, 30.1 rebounds, 13.8 assists, 10.8 steals and 3.7 blocks per game while shooting 46.1% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 64.0 points. ------ The Associated Press created this story using technology provided by Data Skrive and data from Sportradar . I have enough data to make a well-informed pick. Let me synthesize what I've gathered:

**Key findings:**
- **Vanderbilt**: KenPom #12, Torvik #10, 26-7 (26-8 per ESPN after SEC Tournament final loss to Arkansas). AdjO 127.3-127.8 (6th nationally), AdjD 99.2 (28th). Adjusted efficiency margin +28.12. SEC conference, ranked #16 AP. Made SEC Tournament final.
- **McNeese**: KenPom #67, 28-5, Southland Conference champions. AdjO 114.3 (91st), AdjD 101.8. Adjusted efficiency margin +12.52.
- **Spread**: Vanderbilt -11.5, O/U 150.5
- **Efficiency gap**: +28.12 vs +12.52 = ~15.6 point gap per 100 possessions
- **5/12 upset rate**: ~35% historically, but this gap is wider than typical 5/12 matchups
- Vanderbilt's elite offense (6th nationally) vs McNeese's decent but not elite defense
- No major injury concerns surfaced for either team

The 11.5-point spread reflects a wider quality gap than the typical 5/12 matchup, as Vanderbilt's SEC-tested roster—fresh off an SEC Tournament finals run—possesses significantly more firepower than McNeese's Southland Conference champions can match.
